title: EFVP — Loi 25 Privacy Impact Assessments, in public
problem: Loi 25 art. 3.3 requires a Privacy Impact Assessment (EFVP in French) before adopting any third-party processing of personal data. The portal uses two: Sentry (cross-border, US) for error monitoring and Stripe (Canadian entity) for payments. The Loi 25 article doesn't require these PIAs to be public, but keeping them at /pia — instead of 'available on request' — converts a compliance artifact into a transparency move that a buyer in due diligence can read without scheduling a call. The canonical markdown sources live at docs/loi-25-pia.md and docs/loi-25-pia-stripe.md; this page mirrors them faithfully.
analysis: Two PIA records on one page, anchored. Each follows the Loi 25 art. 3.3 template: purpose of the transfer, data categories, recipient + their jurisdiction, retention period, mitigations applied, residual risk acceptance, and annual review schedule. Sentry PIA: error monitoring; the receiving entity is Functional Software Inc. (US); mitigations include the four-part bundle documented in the project's memory (PIA + privacy-page §6 + code minimization + operator checklist). Stripe PIA: payments; the receiving entity is Stripe Payments Canada Ltd. (Canadian processing entity, no cross-border transfer at the legal level even though the parent is US). Each PIA section has its own anchor (#sentry, #stripe) so /confidentialite §6 and §11 deep-link in. The page is bilingual but the technical PIA text is intentionally only in English — that's the language a juriste or the Commission d'accès à l'information would expect for a compliance artifact. The FR intro explains why and what the reader is about to see.
implementation: src/pages/Pia.tsx — single component. COPY constant carries the intro + the heading scaffolding in both languages; the PIA bodies themselves are rendered from a fixed structure (sections with h + p arrays) keyed by the two processors. Anchors are set explicitly (id='sentry', id='stripe') so deep links from /confidentialite work. PageMast + SectionEyebrow + FeatureFolioLink chrome consistent with Privacy.tsx; meta-tinted via PAGE_FEATURE['page.pia']. FeatureContinue at the bottom closes the meta loop (pia → map-page). The 'Dernière mise à jour' date is hardcoded; updates require editing the page AND the corresponding docs/loi-25-pia*.md source.

decisions.decision: Publish PIAs at /pia rather than 'available on request'
decisions.rationale: Loi 25 doesn't require publication, but 'available on request' adds a step that buyers' legal teams routinely skip during evaluation. Publishing converts the compliance artifact into a trust signal — a competitor running cross-border without a PIA looks worse by comparison, and the buyer can verify our claims in 60 seconds instead of an email round-trip. The cost is keeping the page current (annual review per Loi 25, plus on every processor change).
decisions.recommendation: Don't soften the publication to 'available on request' just because the law allows. Keep the page; it earns its keep as a trust signal more than as a compliance artifact.

decisions.decision: PIA technical body in English only, bilingual intro
decisions.rationale: PIAs are read by lawyers, by the Commission d'accès à l'information, and by buyers' diligence teams. All three groups operate in English for technical-legal vocabulary; translating the body would risk introducing legal-meaning drift between FR and EN versions. The intro is bilingual so any FR-only visitor understands what they're looking at and why the body switches language; the canonical markdown sources at docs/ are also English-only.
decisions.recommendation: Don't translate the PIA bodies. If a Loi 25 enforcement action ever requires a French-language version, generate it at that moment with a legal translator, don't maintain two languages in parallel forever.

decisions.decision: Stripe PIA treats Stripe Payments Canada Ltd. as the recipient, not Stripe Inc.
decisions.rationale: Stripe operates a Canadian processing entity that handles all merchant operations originating in Canada; Stripe Inc. (US parent) doesn't receive the personal data at the legal level. Treating the Canadian entity as the recipient is structurally accurate and removes the 'cross-border' framing the buyer would otherwise need to evaluate. The PIA still discloses the parent-subsidiary relationship for full disclosure.
decisions.recommendation: If Stripe ever consolidates Canadian operations into the US entity (publicly announced via Stripe's contracts page), update the PIA to reflect the new flow and re-evaluate the cross-border mitigations.

successCriteria:
- Both PIAs (Sentry, Stripe) are fully rendered and reachable at /pia
- /confidentialite §6 anchors deep-link into the Sentry PIA; §11 into the Stripe PIA
- PIA bodies match the canonical sources in docs/loi-25-pia*.md exactly (no copy-edit drift)
- 'Dernière mise à jour' date reflects the most recent PIA edit
- A buyer in due diligence can answer 'what processors does this site use, what data goes to each, where, and for how long?' without leaving the page
- The page is bilingual at the intro layer; the technical bodies are English-only by design
userGuide: Open /pia (or /en/pia). The intro (FR or EN depending on path) explains why the technical PIA bodies are in English. Below the intro, two PIA sections — Sentry (#sentry) and Stripe (#stripe) — each cover purpose, data categories, recipient + jurisdiction, retention, mitigations, residual risk, and annual review. To update a PIA: edit the corresponding docs/loi-25-pia*.md source first (that's the canonical record), then mirror the change into Pia.tsx, then bump the 'Dernière mise à jour' date. Annual review is scheduled per each PIA — calendar reminders at Marc's level, not automated.
knownLimitations: The PIA bodies are duplicated between Pia.tsx and docs/loi-25-pia*.md — there's no build step that asserts they match; drift is caught only on manual review
Anchors (#sentry, #stripe) are hardcoded — adding a third processor requires extending both Pia.tsx and /confidentialite's link list
Annual review isn't automated; a Loi 25 audit could find an out-of-date PIA if Marc forgets the calendar reminder — known and accepted, the cost-benefit of an automation here is low
Technical body is English-only by design — a French-only Loi 25 inspector would need to read English; the intro explains why this trade-off was made

statusHistory.reason: Page shipped with Sentry PIA; followed by Stripe PIA addition on 2026-05-17.
Code-first doc co-located. Both PIAs live, deep-link from /confidentialite, and mirror their docs/ sources. The publication-as-trust-signal decision held.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P1 sweep, co-located doc for /pia.
